title: Observability Infrastructure: OpenTelemetry, Prometheus Metrics, and Grafana Alloy Integration
description: Implement a shared observability layer in libs/common/telemetry/ that all services (Discovery and Sentinel) import. Covers: structured logging with trace correlation, OpenTelemetry traces (OTLP export), Prometheus metrics, and Grafana Alloy configuration for log/metric collection. Promtail must NOT be used — it reached End-of-Life on March 2, 2026. Use Grafana Alloy instead.
objectives: Unified telemetry setup across all services with a single init call
All logs are structured (JSON), include trace_id and span_id for correlation
Traces exported to OTel Collector via OTLP HTTP
Prometheus metrics accessible at /metrics endpoint on each service
Grafana Alloy collects logs from Docker containers and forwards to Loki

implementation_guidance.overview: libs/common/telemetry/ provides: setup_telemetry(service_name, version, settings) — call once at startup. setup_logging() — configure structlog with JSON formatter and OTel trace injection. get_tracer(name) and get_meter(name) — wrappers around OTel SDK. Prometheus metrics via prometheus_client or opentelemetry-sdk with PrometheusExporter.
steps: Step 1 - TelemetrySettings Pydantic Model: Fields: service_name: str, service_version: str = '0.0.0', otel_endpoint: AnyHttpUrl = 'http://localhost:4318' (OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT), otel_enabled: bool = True, log_level: str = 'INFO', prometheus_enabled: bool = True, prometheus_port: int = 9090 (optional separate server or use /metrics on main app).

Step 2 - OTel Tracer Setup: Use opentelemetry-sdk. OTLPSpanExporter(endpoint=settings.otel_endpoint + '/v1/traces'). Resource attributes: service.name, service.version, deployment.environment. BatchSpanProcessor. TracerProvider registered as global. Instrument FastAPI with FastAPIInstrumentor.instrument() and httpx with HTTPXClientInstrumentor.instrument(). SQLAlchemy: AsyncEngineInstrumentor.instrument().

Step 3 - OTel Metrics Setup: MeterProvider with OTLPMetricExporter(endpoint + '/v1/metrics'). PeriodicExportingMetricReader (interval 30s). For Prometheus: add PrometheusMetricReader to expose /metrics via prometheus_client HTTP server (run in background thread) or mount on FastAPI app.

Step 4 - Structured Logging with structlog: Use structlog 24.x. Configure: structlog.configure(processors=[structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars, structlog.stdlib.add_log_level, structlog.stdlib.add_logger_name, add_otel_trace_context (custom processor), structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt='iso'), structlog.processors.JSONRenderer()]). The add_otel_trace_context processor reads current span's trace_id and span_id and injects them as log fields.

Step 5 - add_otel_trace_context Processor: from opentelemetry import trace; span = trace.get_current_span(); if span.is_recording(): ctx = span.get_span_context(); inject trace_id = format(ctx.trace_id, '032x'); span_id = format(ctx.span_id, '016x') into the event dict.

Step 6 - FastAPI Middleware Integration: RequestLoggingMiddleware: before handler, structlog.contextvars.clear_contextvars(), bind request_id (from X-Request-ID header or generate UUID4), method, path. After handler, log response_time_ms and status_code. This middleware ensures all logs within a request share the same context.

Step 7 - Standard Counters and Histograms: Define in common/telemetry/metrics.py: http_requests_total (counter, labels: method, path, status_code), http_request_duration_seconds (histogram, labels: method, path), sentinel_vc_validations_total (counter, labels: result=valid|invalid|expired), sentinel_replay_cache_hits_total (counter), sentinel_active_instances (gauge). All metrics use the service name as prefix.

Step 8 - Grafana Alloy Configuration (deploy/alloy/config.alloy): Use River syntax (Alloy native config). Define: discovery.docker component to discover running containers. loki.source.docker component to tail container logs. loki.write component pointing to Loki at http://loki:3100. Add labels: {job='docker', container=component.label}. Do NOT use Promtail — Alloy replaces it.

Step 9 - Grafana Alloy Docker Service: Add to docker-compose.yaml: image: grafana/alloy:v1.2.0. Volumes: /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro, ./deploy/alloy/config.alloy:/etc/alloy/config.alloy. Command: alloy run /etc/alloy/config.alloy. Add Loki and Grafana services to docker-compose as well (separate from main stack, in docker-compose.observability.yaml profile).

Step 10 - setup_telemetry() Entry Point: Single function in common/telemetry/__init__.py that configures all of the above. Called in services' main.py or app.py before creating FastAPI app. Accepts TelemetrySettings. Returns (tracer, meter) tuple.
security_constraints: Logs MUST NOT contain: private keys, secret values, raw tokens, credential payloads — use log field sanitizers
OTel spans MUST sanitize span attributes — DID private keys, VP JWT payloads must never be span attributes
Prometheus /metrics endpoint MUST be firewalled — not exposed publicly; internal network only
structlog processor chain MUST include a sanitize_sensitive_fields processor before JSONRenderer
Request bodies MUST NOT be logged by default — opt-in only via DEBUG_LOG_REQUEST_BODY=1 flag in dev env

technical_requirements: Grafana Alloy v1.2+ (NOT Promtail — EOL March 2, 2026)
acceptance_criteria: setup_telemetry() can be imported and called from both services
Log output is valid JSON with trace_id and span_id fields on every line when a request is in flight
GET /metrics returns Prometheus format metrics from any service
OTel Collector receives spans and logs from services when running via docker compose
Grafana Alloy config.alloy is valid River syntax (alloy fmt passes)
No private key or VC payload appears in any log or span attribute in tests
